description: Fix all ML model display issues on brain-monitor page: electrode 4 Error, Sleep/Cog.Load/Meditation showing —, model sklearn fallback failures

userStories.title: Strip channel_quality from non-Muse websocket frames
userStories.description: As a user, I should not see '4 Error' on the Electrode Status Grid when using the Synthetic board. channel_quality is derived from signal statistics, not real electrode impedance — it is only meaningful for Muse devices. For all other boards (synthetic, OpenBCI, unknown), the websocket should not include channel_quality in the frame.
acceptanceCriteria: In ml/api/websocket.py, when building frame['quality'], strip keys channel_quality, channel_scores, channel_details when device is not muse_*
Strip also applies when device_manager.current_device_type is None (backend restarted)
Muse devices (device_type starts with 'muse_') still receive channel_quality
Electrode Status Grid shows 'Quality data not available for this device' for Synthetic board
Electrode Status Grid still shows per-channel quality circles for Muse 2
userStories.notes: Implemented in commit 50f8676. Deployed to Railway.

userStories.title: Add sklearn-to-feature-based fallback in all 10 ML model files
userStories.description: As a user, I should see real values for Cog. Load, Meditation, Sleep, Creativity, Attention, Flow, Dream, Drowsiness, Stress, and Lucid Dream instead of — or 0%. When a model's sklearn .pkl fails (version mismatch, corrupt scaler, missing feature keys), the predict() method should fall through to the feature-based heuristic path instead of propagating an exception that the websocket catches as {}.
acceptanceCriteria: All 10 model files wrap _predict_sklearn() call in try/except with pass to fall through

Files: cognitive_load_estimator.py, meditation_classifier.py, attention_classifier.py, creativity_detector.py, dream_detector.py, drowsiness_detector.py, flow_state_detector.py, lucid_dream_detector.py, sleep_staging.py, stress_detector.py
Cog. Load shows 'low'/'moderate'/'high' instead of — when streaming
Meditation shows 'relaxed'/'meditating'/'deep' instead of — when streaming
userStories.notes: Implemented in commit f00e135. Deployed to Railway.

userStories.title: Wrap sleep model predict in inner try/except in websocket.py
userStories.description: As a user, Sleep staging should show a value (e.g. 'Wake') instead of — when streaming. The sleep model predict() was not wrapped in its own try/except inside the outer analysis block, so a crash would prevent subsequent code from running.
acceptanceCriteria: In websocket.py, sleep model predict() is wrapped in try/except that logs warning and sets sleep_pred = {}
Sleep showing 'Wake' or other stage when Synthetic board is streaming
userStories.notes: Implemented in commit 0988f1e. Deployed to Railway.

userStories.title: Verify all model outputs show real values in live UI
userStories.description: As a user, after all backend fixes are deployed, I should see real values across all model cards on the brain-monitor page when the Synthetic board is streaming. No card should show — unless no frame has been received yet.
acceptanceCriteria: Electrode grid shows 'Simulated data' or 'Quality data not available' (no '4 Error')
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verification story — run after Railway deploy completes (~2 min after each commit).
